zh.lifeSummary: 當Martha de Béarn於1245年出生在法國時，她的父親Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn24歲，母親Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre17歲。她於1260年與Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1317年6月6日之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Martha de Béarn于1245年出生在法国时，她的父亲Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn24岁，母亲Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre17岁。她于1260年与Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1317年6月6日之后。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Béarn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname for a bearded man (from Middle English berd, Old English beard). To be clean-shaven was the norm in non-Jewish communities in northwestern Europe from the 12th to the 16th century, the crucial period for surname formation. There is a placename and other evidence to show that this word was used as a byname in the Old English period, when beards were the norm; in this period the byname would have referred to a large or noticeable beard. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, in particular German Barth 1.

habitational name from a place called Beard in Derbyshire (now represented by Beard Hall and Beardwood Farms in New Mills parish), which derives its name by dissimilation from Old English brerd ‘rim, bank’.

variants.name: Bardwell
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from Bardwell in Suffolk, so named with an unattested Old English byname Bearda, a derivative of beard (see Beard ) + Old English well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Alternatively, the first element may be from a dissimilated form of Old English bre(o)rd ‘brim, bank’.

variants.name: Barth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Franz, Erwin, Florian, Gerhard, Gunther, Klaus, Manfred, Armin, Arno.

German and Swiss German: nickname for a bearded man, from Middle High German bart ‘beard’. This name, which can not be always distinguished from 2 and 4 below, is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and Slovenia, in the last four countries sometimes as a German translation of Slavic surnames meaning ‘beard’ or ‘bearded man’ (see Bart 1). See also Beard 1.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Pomerania.

variants.name: Bard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Aime, Alphonse, Alphy, Armand, Cecile, Celestine, Emile, Fernand, Germaine, Gilles, Jacques.

English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Old French personal name Bard(e), ancient Germanic Bardo, from barta ‘battle axe’. This was borne as a surname by a prominent Norman family with lands in west Normandy and in various English and Scottish counties, including Essex, Hertfordshire, North Yorkshire, Northumberland, and Lanarkshire. In Scotland the original family were Norman landowners in Strathaven parish in Lanarkshire. They were descended from the Baards, lords of Loftus in the North Yorkshire, descendants of the mid 12th-century Richard Baart.
Irish: altered form of either   Beard   or   Baird  .

variants.name: Bearb
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aurelien, Camille, Micheline, Pierre.
Altered form of English Beard , under the influence, in LA, of French barbe ‘beard’.

nameMeanings.name: Martha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, of Aramaic rather than Hebrew origin, meaning ‘lady’. It was borne by the sister of Lazarus and Mary of Bethany (John 11:1). According to Luke 10:38, when Jesus visited the house of Mary and Martha, Mary sat at his feet, listening to him, while Martha ‘was cumbered about much serving’, so that she complained to Jesus, ‘Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone?’ For this reason, the name Martha has always been associated with hard domestic work, as opposed to the contemplative life.

en.lifeSummary: When Martha de Béarn was born in 1245, in France, her father, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, was 24 and her mother, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, was 17. She married Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac in 1260. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died after 6 June 1317.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Martha de Béarn est née en 1245, à France, son père, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac en 1260. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée après 6 juin 1317.

de.lifeSummary: Als Martha de Béarn 1245 in Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn 24 und ihre Mutter Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre 17. Sie hat 1260 Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb nach 6. Juni 1317.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Martha de Béarn è nata il nel 1245 nel Francia, sua padre, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac nel 1260. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì dopo il 6 giugno 1317.

ja.lifeSummary: Martha de Béarnが1245年にフランスで生まれた時，父Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarnは24歳，母Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorreは17歳であった。1260年にGerald VI Comte d'Armagnacと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1317年6月6日以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Martha de Béarn(이)가 1245년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1260년 Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1317년 6월 6일 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Martha de Béarn nasceu em 1245, em França, seu pai, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac em 1260. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu após 6 de junho de 1317.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Martha de Béarn родилась в 1245 г. в местности Франция, её отцу по имени Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac в 1260 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла после 6 июнь 1317 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Martha de Béarn nació en 1245, en Francia, su papá, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Gerald VI Comte d'Armagnac en 1260. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió después de 6 de junio de 1317.
